content.problemStatement:
You are given nnn sets S1,S2,‚Ä¶,SnS_1, S_2, \ldots, S_nS1‚Äč,S2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,Sn‚Äč.

For each iii, you want to choose xi‚ąąSix_i \in S_ixi‚Äč‚ąąSi‚Äč, such that the variance of the  multiset {x1,x2,‚Ä¶,xn}\{x_1, x_2, \ldots, x_n\}{x1‚Äč,x2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,xn‚Äč} is minimized.

The variance of a multiset {x1,x2,‚Ä¶,xn}\{x_1, x_2, \ldots, x_n\}{x1‚Äč,x2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,xn‚Äč} is defined as ŌÉ2=‚ąĎi=1n(xi‚ąíőľ)2n\sigma^2 = \displaystyle  \dfrac{\sum_{i=1}^{n} (x_i - \mu)^2}{n} ŌÉ2=n‚ąĎi=1n‚Äč(xi‚Äč‚ąíőľ)2‚Äč, where őľ\muőľ is the mean, which equals ‚ąĎi=1nxin\displaystyle \dfrac{ \sum_{i=1}^{n}x_i}{n}n‚ąĎi=1n‚Äčxi‚Äč‚Äč.

Say the minimum possible variance is RRR. Print n2√óRn^2 \times Rn2√óR. It can be proved that n2Rn^2 Rn2R is always an integer.
constraints: All elements in a single set are distinct.
The elements of all the sets are between 111 and 50,00050,00050,000 inclusive
